summary: Provision a brand-new Timestream time-series database. No Timestream estate exists yet, so this is a first stand-up an engineer reviews before it applies.

operations.title: Provision a new Timestream database
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Timestream time-series database; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a time-series database as a complete specification: name and encryption key. Tables (with their own retention properties) are added afterward as an engineer follow-up. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: database_name
params.label: Database name
params.help: Letters, numbers, underscore, dash and dot. Names the database in Timestream.

params.name: kms_key
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Leave blank to use the default AWS-managed key. Pick a key only for a customer-managed CMK.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Database name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this database holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this database.

decisions: Tables, with their own memory/magnetic-store retention properties, are an engineer follow-up after the database exists
